label: neither
rationale: Defensive assertion 'nobody has ever won a war against Kikuyus' in response to persecution claims - ethnic pride rather than contempt toward others.

label: neither
rationale: Reporting that Maasai are saying they will block/not leave - descriptive statement of protest action.

label: hate
rationale: 'Madem wajaluo na mechi ni kama kasongo na uogo' - mocking Luo women collectively, attributing cowardice as group characteristic.

label: neither
rationale: Historical discussion of racism and colonial dynamics in Buenos Aires, not expressing hate itself.

label: neither
rationale: 'Ruto has no competitor, others are noise' - political prediction with no ethnic dimension.

label: neither
rationale: Warning about rhetoric risking repeat of 2008 violence - reporting on threat rather than making one.

rationale: 'Joluo must grow brains' - collective attribution of lacking intelligence to Luo community as a group.

label: hate
rationale: 'Cut off all the heads of this Islamic Medusa' - violent eliminationist imagery targeting religious group.

label: offensive
rationale: 'thief and killer' aimed at Ruto, 'fala' at Itumbi - personal insults of individuals, not group-based.

label: neither
rationale: Accusing individual Sifuna of hating Kikuyus - statement about one person's alleged attitude, not expressing hate.

label: hate
rationale: 'They are a cancer ruining the advancement of our ngubu society' - dehumanizing language applied to ethnic categories including Bantu/Kavirondo references.

label: offensive
rationale: 'the mullahs' and 'fools' - criticism of Iranian religious/political leaders as individuals, not group attack.

label: neither
rationale: 'that tribalist can't stand with votes' - political criticism of named individuals' electoral viability.

label: hate
rationale: 'we know you Luhyas, ukishiba you are okay' - collective stereotype that Luhya people are easily satisfied/bought.

label: neither
rationale: Joke about needing welding goggles because Linda Mwananchi's future is bright - political humor.

label: offensive
rationale: 'Wakenya wengine wajinga' - calling some Kenyans stupid for supporting Argentina, no ethnic targeting.

label: neither
rationale: 'so Kikuyus are preventing police from getting goons?' - rhetorical question about political dynamics, not assertion.

label: hate
rationale: 'they want Kalonzo someone whom they will manipulate' - collective attribution of manipulative intent to Kikuyus as group.

label: neither
rationale: 'Kikuyus won't see this seat soon' - electoral prediction about voting bloc, no contempt.

rationale: Discussion of Kipsigis and Nandi as neutral dialects in Rift Valley politics - linguistic observation.

rationale: 'maskondee would have killed people to get power' - accusation of political violence intent, directed at individual/faction.

rationale: 'Kahaba na mlevi' (prostitute and drunkard) - crude personal insults without protected characteristic.

label: neither
rationale: Accusing president of having 'deeper hate for Kikuyus' - describing someone else's alleged hate, not expressing it.

rationale: 'watu wa tutam have a special place in hell' - consigning Luhya community to damnation collectively.

rationale: 'He can't remove Kambas where Kalonzo is' - ethnic-bloc political analysis, no attack.
